title: Preparation and transformation of chemically super-competent Escherichia coli
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fepkl","text":"Based on a method produced by Inoue, et al.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eahup","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"31ob9","text":"Inoue, Nojima, H., \u0026 Okayama, H. (1990). High efficiency transformation of Escherichia coli with plasmids.Gene,96(1), 23–28. https://doi.org/10.1016/0378-1119(90)90336-P","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":106,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":111,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":125,"length":44}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"2AE7ACC0A97411EDAEF39B3629F312BA","url":"https://doi.org/10.1016/0378-1119(90)90336-P"}}}}
steps: In a sterile flask, add 200 mL distilled water
Quickly thaw a single reaction tube with 100 µL hyper-competent cells in Inoue-DMSO

Measure 2.5 mL ( 1 Molarity (M) ) PIPES, 2.177 g Manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate, 3.75 mL ( 1 Molarity (M) ) Calcium chloride and 4.660 g Potassium chloride. Materials: PIPES Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P1851 Manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #M3634 Calcium chloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C3881 Potassium chloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P4504
Filter sterilize solution with a filter (0.2 µm) and store refrigerated ( 4 °C )
Fill flask with distilled water to 250 mL
Adjust pH to pH 6.7 with Potassium hydroxide solution Materials: Potassium hydroxide solution Supelco Catalog #P4494 (CAS: 1310-58-3)
Add measured reagents and mix for 00:05:00

Prepare a culture of E. coli on an LB agar plate. Pick a single colony, and inoculate in 500 mL S. O. C. broth in a 1000 mL flask. Incubate at 18 °C with shaking at 100 rpm overnight, until OD 600 reaches 0.6
Incubate cells on ice for 00:10:00
Discard supernatant and resuspend with 16 mL CRM transformation buffer
Centrifuge tubes with 5000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Place tubes on ice for 00:10:00
Aliquot entire culture volume into 50 mL canonical tubes
Discard supernatant and resuspend with 10 mL DMSO-Inoue per tube

Centrifuge tubes with 5000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes . Meanwhile, prepare 50 mL DMSO-Inoue transformation buffer by diluting 3.5 mL DMSO in 46.5 mL Inoue transformation buffer Materials: Dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) Catalog #196055 (CAS: 67-68-5)
Centrifuge tubes with 5000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Discard supernatant and resuspend with 8 mL Inoue transformation buffer. Pool into two tubes
Snap freeze tubes in liquid nitrogen using a floating foam tube rack (Southern labware #HS2166)
Aliquot 100 µL cell suspension into sterile 500 µL screw cap reaction tubes (Sarstedt #72.704.200). While creating aliquots, keep original tubes, and aliquots on ice, until snap-freeze take place
Incubate cells on ice for 00:30:00
Transfer aliquots storage box, and place in an ultra-low temperature freezer or vapor-phase nitrogen tank Store tubes in 50 mL canonical tubes, or similar containers
Incubate cells on ice for 00:30:00
Mix 1-5 µL plasmid (ligation product) Do not exceed 5% of the volume competent cells Use up-to 25 ng per 50 µL of competent cells
Heat-shock cells at 42 °C for 00:00:30 , followed by 00:02:00 at 4 °C immediatly
Add 500 µL prewarmed S. O. C. medium and incubate for 37 °C at 200 rpm for 01:00:00
Add desired amount of suspension on LB plates with ampicillin (100 µg/mL) and incubate overnight
creator: Andreas Sagen
